## Chapter 17

**Paragraph 1:**

~~After a brief cut to Ichi drinking and wrestling with jealousy over Motoko's impossible skill, the story returns to the badlands. After a brief cut to Ichi drinking and wrestling with jealousy over Motoko's impossible skill, the story returns to the badlands. Motoko loots goggles and a dust mask off a dead Raffen, staggers through the storm to the wayhouse, and ambushes Connor at gunpoint the moment he stumbles inside. He's not a traitor — Scorpion was simply late for their meet. He gives her the GPS coords for the Wraith camp. She spends her stat points and walks into the Raffen compound alone. ## Chapter 39

**Paragraph 1:**

~~With Wakako's jobs cleared, Motoko sits with the revenge question for the first time since Adaptation made her feel human again. The burning hatred for the Spider Ripper and the Bastard is still there — but she decides it won't consume her life. She will hunt them when the opportunity comes. They won't take her life on top of her arms. She shelves the obsession, turns to her robotics design project. Arasaka CAD plus Engineering unlocked a whole new dimension: she starts drafting a spider-drone concept loosely based on the Flathead, quickly discovers designing a robot is far harder than she assumed. Tachikoma dreams vs. reality: her first attempt is embarrassing.~~

With Wakako's jobs cleared, Motoko sits with the revenge question for the first time since Adaptation made her feel human again. The burning hatred for the Spider Ripper and the Bastard is still there — but she decides it won't consume her life. She will hunt them when the opportunity comes. They won't take her life on top of her arms. She shelves the obsession, turns to her robotics design project. Arasaka CAD plus Engineering unlocked a whole new dimension: she starts drafting a spider-drone concept loosely based on the Flathead, quickly discovers designing a robot is far harder than she assumed. Tachikoma dreams vs. Reality: her first attempt is embarrassing.

The Ghost Touch bypass of V3L0CITY's server takes all day but works without triggering any kill switches. All data copied and drives wiped; the setup reconfigured for Motoko, each camera isolated into its own network partition. Crew gets elevator access keys. Richard does not. Jun celebrates her 'taking over a gang' despite her protests. That night she loads V3L's programs into her deck — ICE Shield, HELL FLAME, Ping, Barrier Dataload — dons the Netrunner suit, and dives for real.

She discovers the Kabuki Roundabout server is the local netrunner hub — the Dewdrop Inn's real face on the net — and meets a group of teen netrunners bartering code.~~

The net opens as a full rendered city-scape. She explores Megabuilding H4's server and gets attacked by a territorial netrunner named Aaron. She deflects his hack, redirects his assault through a lagged server partition full of someone's massive porn download, then cracks his ICE with HELL FLAME and walls him out with Barrier Dataload until he disconnects. Gained. She discovers the Kabuki Roundabout server is the local netrunner hub — the Dewdrop Inn's real face on the net — and meets a group of teen netrunners bartering code.

SPD responds to the stolen vehicle. Motoko ditches the key and walks away clean.

Revenge raid: Short Circuit locks down the gang, Motoko vaults the shelves and katana-kills the first one out of a couch. Malcolm's smart rounds drop another through the doorway. Rebecca's HMG detonates through the wall, shredding the rest. Motoko drags the leader out and drops him in front of the Minotaur — Hiromi stomps him flat. Remaining Tinos watch from a distance; Motoko confronts them bare-faced, explains it was business retaliation for pulling iron first, invokes the Padre contract. Gang war narrowly averted. Section 9 loots everything back plus extra.

Yoko provides six-month-old security data and wants Militech's new Paraline units in exchange for her assistance; Motoko wants top-end drone hard drives for the Tachikoma. Both agree the showroom's underground access is the better target over hitting a convoy. Yoko hints that Motoko has never tested herself against corporate ICE despite now having the skill. The chapter closes with band practice: the plan to record an official studio version of Faint is set for that night.

## Chapter 234

**Paragraph 2:**

She leverages the meeting cleverly: she asks if MSM has any Militech connections, and when Ian confirms subsidiary deals, she requests access to the Militech Showroom as a 'personal interest' — getting herself an official excuse to scout the location she plans to rob. Ian is pleased with the trade and sets it up. Afterward, Motoko tells Jun about the Militech heist plan; Jun is instantly and enthusiastically in. Motoko realizes she'll need to scope the showroom, probe Militech's network security, and acquire specialist equipment — and starts planning in earnest. The chapter ends with her dreading the reality of having a scheduled music job like a normal employee.
